headline: 250 years of American independence: A disenchanted anniversary
subtitle: 'America 250' (13/13). Trump's two terms in office are undermining national cohesion. The ideal of equality enshrined in the founding documents is a distant prospect, the system of checks and balances has cracked and any reform seems impossible. Americans' belief in their shared history is eroding. Even the nature of the system of government is a matter of debate. But historians are trying to put things into perspective.

headline: Devastating heatwave leaves France's wildlife in crisis

US gripped by extreme heatwave, disrupting 250th anniversary and World Cup celebrations
subtitle: Temperatures neared 40°C (104°F) in New York, Philadelphia and Washington DC, on Thursday and Friday, causing widespread power outages and prompting organizers to delay the start of Independence Day celebrations.

headline: Pope urges US to recommit to tradition of welcoming migrants on nation's 250th anniversary
subtitle: On the eve of the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, history's first US-born pope focused on America's tradition of welcoming migrants, whom he referred to as part of the 'noble vision' of the Founding Fathers.

headline: With 'Confessions II,' Madonna celebrates the pleasures of partying, blending glittering hedonism and hypnotic power
subtitle: By returning to the dance floor, the American singer finds her comfort zone again on her 15th album and, in the process, reveals a more vulnerable side.

headline: The incredible success of the de Gaulle biopic 'reflects the paradox embodied by the hero of Free France'
subtitle: The first installment of La Bataille de Gaulle, released on June 3, got off to a disappointing start before box office numbers soared, driven by a historic heatwave and word of mouth. This is especially significant, as the film resonates with current events, writes Le Monde's culture columnist.

subtitle: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

headline: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subtitle: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.
